TSLA: gross margin

8 quarter(s) searched · 186 filing section(s) searched · 8 period(s) with evidence
Credibility timeline
Q1 2022Q2 2022Q3 2022Q4 2022Q1 2023Q2 2023Q3 2023Q4 2023
guidance met/exceededguidance missedguidance given, not yet resolvedno forecast data this quarter

Longitudinal Summary

The gross margin performance evolved over the period, starting with a significant increase in Q1 2022, followed by fluctuations and declines in subsequent quarters. Despite management's positive outlook and efforts to improve margins, there were challenges in maintaining gross margins, leading to a downward trend by Q4 2023.

Forecasting Credibility: Medium

Management's forecasting credibility is rated as Medium due to a mix of accurate and inaccurate predictions regarding gross margins over the period. While they accurately forecasted an improvement in Q4 2022, they failed to anticipate the significant decrease in Q1 2023 and the continued decline in subsequent quarters.

Revenue & gross margin trend
Revenue ($18.76B → $96.77B) Gross margin (29.1% → 18.2%)
Q1 2022 – Q4 2023

Trends

  • Initial increase in gross margin in Q1 2022
  • Fluctuations and declines in gross margin in later quarters

Recurring Themes / Risks

  • Efforts to improve margins through cost reductions and deal economics
  • Challenges in maintaining gross margins due to various factors

Key Concerns

  • Inconsistencies in forecasting accuracy
  • Potential impact of external factors like tariffs on gross margins

Conclusion

The evidence suggests that the company initially saw success in improving gross margins but struggled to sustain this performance over time. The fluctuations and declines in gross margins raise concerns about the company's ability to effectively manage costs and maintain profitability. The management's forecasting credibility is rated as Medium, given the mixed track record of accurately predicting gross margin performance.

By Reporting Period

Q1 2022

What management said

  • "Last quarter, we demonstrated a series of new financial records, including revenue, gross margins, operating margin and bottom line profitability."
  • "And thus, those inefficiencies will start to flow through our gross margins in Q2."
  • "GAAP automotive gross margin reached 32.9% and first time exceeded 30% when excluding regulatory credits."
  • "As a result of our ongoing improvements in operating leverage, we achieved a record operating margin of over 19%."

Guidance given

No forward guidance identified this period.

Actual reported

  • from 10-K/10-Q"Gross margin for total automotive increased from 26.5% in the three months ended March 31, 2021 to 32.9% in the three months ended March 31, 2022."
  • "GAAP automotive gross margin reached 32.9% and first time exceeded 30% when excluding regulatory credits."

Filing disclosures (10-K/10-Q)

  • jump to section"Gross margin for total automotive increased from 26.5% in the three months ended March 31, 2021 to 32.9% in the three months ended March 31, 2022."
  • jump to section"Gross margin for total automotive & services and other segment increased from 23.4% in the three months ended March 31, 2021 to 30.5% in the three months ended March 31, 2022, primarily due to the automotive gross margin impacts discussed above and an improvement in our services and other gross margin."
  • jump to section"Gross margin for energy generation and storage increased from -20.4% in the three months ended March 31, 2021 to -11.7% in the three months ended March 31, 2022, primarily due to higher deployments of Powerwall which operated at a higher gross margin as well as an improvement in Solar Roof gross margin due to reductions in average costs per unit."

+21 more table(s) in this filing, not shown.

Q2 2022

What management said

  • "On GAAP automotive gross margin, it declined sequentially to 27.9%."
  • "Additionally, our energy business achieved record gross profit with the highest solar volumes in many years."

Guidance given

No forward guidance identified this period.

Actual reported

  • from 10-K/10-Q"Gross margin for total automotive increased from 27.5% to 30.6% in the six months ended June 30, 2022 as compared to the six months ended June 30, 2021."
  • "On GAAP automotive gross margin, it declined sequentially to 27.9%."

Filing disclosures (10-K/10-Q)

+3 more table(s) in this filing, not shown.

  • jump to section"Gross margin for total automotive decreased from 28.4% to 27.9% in the three months ended June 30, 2022 as compared to the three months ended June 30, 2021."
  • jump to section"Gross margin for total automotive increased from 27.5% to 30.6% in the six months ended June 30, 2022 as compared to the six months ended June 30, 2021."
  • jump to section"Gross margin for total automotive & services and other segment increased from 24.6% to 28.3% in the six months ended June 30, 2022 as compared to the six months ended June 30, 2021, primarily due to the automotive gross margin impacts discussed above and an improvement in our services and other gross margin."
  • jump to section"Gross margin for total automotive & services and other segment stayed flat at 25.7% in the three months ended June 30, 2022 as compared to the three months ended June 30, 2021."
  • jump to section"Additionally, services and other was a higher percentage of the segment gross margin during the three months and six months ended June 30, 2022 as compared to the prior year."

+21 more table(s) in this filing, not shown.

Q3 2022

What management said

  • "On automotive profitability, our GAAP operating margin was 17.2% with automotive gross margin at 27.9%."
  • "Removing regulatory credits and Austin and Berlin, our operating margins would have been our strongest yet and auto gross margin would have been nearly 30%."
  • "Operating margin is one of our best yet, with improvements in operating leverage."
  • "On energy profitability, we achieved our strongest gross profit yet for this business, driven primarily by record volumes of our Megapack and Powerwall products."
  • "We had our industry-leading operating margin reach 17%."

Guidance given

No forward guidance identified this period.

Actual reported

No actual results identified this period.

Filing disclosures (10-K/10-Q)

+4 more table(s) in this filing, not shown.

  • jump to section"Gross margin for total automotive & services and other segment increased from 26.0% to 27.4% in the nine months ended September 30, 2022 as compared to the nine months ended September 30, 2021, primarily due to the automotive gross margin increase discussed above and an improvement in our services and other gross margin."
  • jump to section"Gross margin for total automotive decreased from 30.5% to 27.9% in the three months ended September 30, 2022 as compared to the three months ended September 30, 2021."
  • jump to section"Gross margin for total automotive increased from 28.6% to 29.6% in the nine months ended September 30, 2022 as compared to the nine months ended September 30, 2021."
  • jump to section"Gross margin for total automotive & services and other segment decreased from 28.2% to 26.0% in the three months ended September 30, 2022 as compared to the three months ended September 30, 2021, primarily due to the automotive gross margin decrease discussed above and offset by an improvement in our services and other gross margin."
  • jump to section"Additionally, services and other was a higher percentage of the segment gross margin during the three and nine months ended September 30, 2022 as compared to the prior year."

+21 more table(s) in this filing, not shown.

Q4 2022

What management said

  • "Where do you see average selling price and gross margins after the price cuts?"
  • "So based upon these metrics here, we believe that we'll be above both of the metrics that are stated in the question, so 20% automotive gross margin, excluding leases and rent credits and then $47,000 ASP across all models."
  • "So that means that there's millions of cars were full self-driving can be sold at essentially 100% gross margin."
  • "The next question from investors is, after recent price cuts, analyst released expectations that Tesla automotive gross margin, excluding leasing and credits, will drop below 20% and average selling price around $47,000 across all models."
  • "The second comment I wanted to make here is that as a management team here, we're most focused on what our operating margin is."
  • "Thus, there will be an impact on operating margin in the near term."
  • "And so as other areas of the business become more important, particularly the energy business, which is growing faster than the vehicle business and as we're heavily focused on operating leverage here, improving efficiency of our overheads, we think the right metric for us to be focused on is operating margin."

Guidance given

  • "So we expect to see deflation in our input costs most likely, which would then lead to, yes, better margin."

Actual reported

  • Metfrom 10-K/10-Qresolves TSLA_Q4_2022 guidance "Gross margin for energy generation and storage increased from -4.6% to 7.4% in the year ended December 31, 2022 as compared to the year ended December 31, 2021."
  • "So based upon these metrics here, we believe that we'll be above both of the metrics that are stated in the question, so 20% automotive gross margin, excluding leases and rent credits and then $47,000 ASP across all models."

Filing disclosures (10-K/10-Q)

MD&A

+4 more table(s) in this filing, not shown.

  • jump to section"Gross margin for total automotive decreased from 29.3% to 28.5% in the year ended December 31, 2022 as compared to the year ended December 31, 2021."
  • jump to section"Gross margin for total automotive & services and other segment decreased from 26.9% to 26.5% in the year ended December 31, 2022 as compared to the year ended December 31, 2021, primarily due to the automotive gross margin decrease discussed above, partially offset by an improvement in our services and other gross margin."
  • jump to section"Additionally, services and other was a higher percentage of the segment gross margin during the year ended 2022 as compared to the prior year."
  • jump to section"Gross margin for energy generation and storage increased from -4.6% to 7.4% in the year ended December 31, 2022 as compared to the year ended December 31, 2021."
  • jump to section"Additionally, there was a higher proportion of energy storage sales, which operated at a higher gross margin, within the segment."

+37 more table(s) in this filing, not shown.

Q1 2023

What management said

  • "Back on the automotive gross margins."
  • "We generally look to mid-20% gross margins for any program that we launch."
  • "First, automotive gross margin and operating margin reduced sequentially."
  • "The next question is, what do you anticipate 2023 automotive gross margins ex credits will be at the company's current pricing levels?"
  • "So, I think, I guess, a few months ago, even after major price cuts, you felt pretty strongly that 20% automotive gross margin was still probably a reasonable floor."
  • "In particular, automotive gross margin was impacted by a few factors since our discussion on the last earnings call, which include additional action taken in the second half of the quarter to improve vehicle pricing and one-time items, most notably warranty adjustments on older S and X vehicles as well as increased deferred revenue for certain Autopilot features as we transition technologies."
  • "But it's difficult to say what the margin will be."
  • "Just one other thing I wanted to mention on margin."
  • "We are also making progress on storage profitability, generating our highest gross profit yet in the quarter."
  • "And while we reduced prices considerably in early Q1, it's worth noting that our operating margin remains among the best in the industry."

Guidance given

No forward guidance identified this period.

Actual reported

  • from 10-K/10-Q"Gross margin for total automotive decreased from 32.9% to 21.1% in the three months ended March 31, 2023 as compared to the three months ended March 31, 2022."
  • from 10-K/10-Q"Gross margin for energy generation and storage improved from -11.7% to 11.0% in the three months ended March 31, 2023 as compared to the three months ended March 31, 2022."
  • "In particular, automotive gross margin was impacted by a few factors since our discussion on the last earnings call, which include additional action taken in the second half of the quarter to improve vehicle pricing and one-time items, most notably warranty adjustments on older S and X vehicles as well as increased deferred revenue for certain Autopilot features as we transition technologies."

Filing disclosures (10-K/10-Q)

MD&A

  • jump to section"Gross margin for total automotive decreased from 32.9% to 21.1% in the three months ended March 31, 2023 as compared to the three months ended March 31, 2022."
  • jump to section"Gross margin for total automotive & services and other segment decreased from 30.5% to 19.9% in the three months ended March 31, 2023 as compared to the three months ended March 31, 2022, primarily due to the automotive gross margin decrease discussed above, partially offset by an improvement in our services and other gross margin."
  • jump to section"Additionally, services and other was a higher percentage of the segment gross margin during the first quarter of 2023 as compared to the prior year."
  • jump to section"Gross margin for energy generation and storage improved from -11.7% to 11.0% in the three months ended March 31, 2023 as compared to the three months ended March 31, 2022."
  • jump to section"Additionally, there was a higher proportion of energy storage sales, which operated at a higher gross margin, within the segment."

+16 more table(s) in this filing, not shown.

Q2 2023

What management said

  • "If we look specifically at our automotive business, our gross margin showed a modest reduction and remained healthy, despite action taken to further improve vehicle affordability early in the quarter."
  • "With the emphasis of price cuts to drive volume growth eating into automotive gross margin, can investors expect to see automotive gross margin stabilize or even rise due to efficiencies outpacing the cuts?"
  • "If I may, look, the short-term variances in gross margin and profitability really are minor relative to the long-term picture."
  • "For our energy business, we improved margins and gross profit driven by cost reductions and deal economics, particularly with Megapack."
  • "And nonetheless, we managed to achieve operating margin of about 10%."

Guidance given

No forward guidance identified this period.

Actual reported

  • "If we look specifically at our automotive business, our gross margin showed a modest reduction and remained healthy, despite action taken to further improve vehicle affordability early in the quarter."
  • "For our energy business, we improved margins and gross profit driven by cost reductions and deal economics, particularly with Megapack."

Filing disclosures (10-K/10-Q)

MD&A

+2 more table(s) in this filing, not shown.

  • jump to section"Gross margin for total automotive decreased from 27.9% to 19.2% in the three months ended June 30, 2023 as compared to the three months ended June 30, 2022."
  • jump to section"Gross margin for total automotive decreased from 30.6% to 20.1% in the six months ended June 30, 2023 as compared to the six months ended June 30, 2022."
  • jump to section"Gross margin for total automotive & services and other segment decreased from 28.3% to 19.0% in the six months ended June 30, 2023 as compared to the six months ended June 30, 2022, primarily due to the automotive gross margin decrease discussed above, partially offset by an improvement in our services and other gross margin."
  • jump to section"Gross margin for total automotive & services and other segment decreased from 25.7% to 18.2% in the three months ended June 30, 2023 as compared to the three months ended June 30, 2022."
  • jump to section"Additionally, there was a higher proportion of services and other, which operated at a lower gross margin, within the segment during the first half of 2023 as compared to the prior year."

+16 more table(s) in this filing, not shown.

Q3 2023

What management said

  • "So, if you can give us the background on that and tell us how we should think about that gross margin going forward."
  • "It’s about like your gross margin in the quarter."
  • "Could you give us a sense of like in how the gross margin evolved sequentially, how much was the impact of idle cost?"
  • "And they actually contributed pretty well to the margin for this quarter."

Guidance given

No forward guidance identified this period.

Actual reported

  • "As this business grows, the energy division is becoming our highest margin business."

Filing disclosures (10-K/10-Q)

+2 more table(s) in this filing, not shown.

  • jump to section"Gross margin for total automotive decreased from 29.6% to 19.7% in the nine months ended September 30, 2023 as compared to the nine months ended September 30, 2022."
  • jump to section"Gross margin for total automotive & services and other segment decreased from 27.4% to 18.5% in the nine months ended September 30, 2023 as compared to the nine months ended September 30, 2022, primarily due to the automotive gross margin decrease discussed above while there was an improvement in our services and other gross margin."
  • jump to section"Gross margin for total automotive & services and other segment decreased from 26.0% to 17.4% in the three months ended September 30, 2023 as compared to the three months ended September 30, 2022."
  • jump to section"31 Table of Contents Gross margin for total automotive decreased from 27.9% to 18.7% in the three months ended September 30, 2023 as compared to the three months ended September 30, 2022."
  • jump to section"The increases were driven by an improvement in our Megapack gross margin from lower average cost per MWh and a higher proportion of Megapack, which operated at a higher gross margin, within the segment as compared to the prior year periods."

+16 more table(s) in this filing, not shown.

Q4 2023

What management said

  • "That said, predicting auto gross margins is extremely challenging since there are many moving parts to this equation, some of which are out of our control like the change in tariffs or local incentives to name a few."
  • "As a result, our auto gross margin improved sequentially."
  • "The next question is, what is your expectation for automotive gross margin ex-regulatory credits for the full year?"

Guidance given

No forward guidance identified this period.

Actual reported

  • "As a result, our auto gross margin improved sequentially."

Filing disclosures (10-K/10-Q)

+2 more table(s) in this filing, not shown.

  • jump to section"Gross margin for total automotive decreased from 28.5% to 19.4% in the year ended December 31, 2023 as compared to the year ended December 31, 2022."
  • jump to section"Gross margin for total automotive & services and other segment decreased from 26.5% to 18.2% in the year ended December 31, 2023 as compared to the year ended December 31, 2022, primarily due to the automotive gross margin decrease discussed above."
  • jump to section"The increase was driven by an improvement in our Megapack gross margin from lower average cost per MWh and a higher proportion of Megapack, which operated at a higher gross margin, within the segment as compared to the prior year periods."
  • jump to section"40 Gross margin for energy generation and storage increased from 7.4% to 18.9% in the year ended December 31, 2023 as compared to the year ended December 31, 2022."
